title: ‘Reservoir Dogs’ at 30 and the Legacy of Sundance ’92
format: Sean Fennessey and Adam Nayman mark 30 years since 'Reservoir Dogs' premiered at Sundance, then tour the rest of the class of '92 it broke out from.

films.title: Reservoir Dogs
films.note: Funded after Tarantino spent years shopping scripts around Hollywood; Lawrence Bender and Harvey Keitel got it made, shot on a tiny budget in Highland Park in the summer of '91.

films.title: Pulp Fiction
films.note: The one Fennessey actually saw first, as a teenager. The two films are 'kissing cousins,' but Reservoir Dogs works by withholding what Pulp Fiction eventually shows.

films.title: Natural Born Killers
films.note: One of the scripts Tarantino was shopping around Hollywood in the years before Reservoir Dogs got made.

films.title: True Romance
films.note: Sold around the same time as Natural Born Killers, before Tarantino could get Reservoir Dogs financed.

films.title: City on Fire
films.note: The Ringo Lam heist film critics who knew it accused Reservoir Dogs of lifting from.

films.title: The Killing
films.note: The Kubrick heist picture critics of the era read as Reservoir Dogs' clearest antecedent.

films.title: Blue Velvet
films.note: Invoked via David Foster Wallace's line: Lynch is interested in a human ear, Tarantino just wants to show it cut off.

films.title: Terminator 2: Judgment Day
films.note: The counterexample: a movie with nothing obscure to chase down, unlike Reservoir Dogs' cultivated deep cuts.

films.title: The Taking of Pelham One Two Three
films.note: One of the reference points a kid could plausibly have already seen, making the Mr. Pink/Mr. Blonde naming scheme legible.

films.title: Sleep with Me
films.note: Tarantino's cameo riffing on Top Gun, an early sample of the pop-culture monologue he'd become known for.

films.title: Crimson Tide
films.note: An uncredited Tarantino dialogue polish job.

films.title: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
films.note: Cited as proof Tarantino always loved Hollywood, even while he was hustling to break into it.

films.title: Husbands
films.note: The Cassavetes film Buscemi, in some alternate 1967, could plausibly have starred in.

segments.heading: The Legacy of Sundance '92
segments.note: the alumni class, then and now

films.title: In the Soup
films.note: Beat Reservoir Dogs to the Grand Jury Prize that year, carried by Steve Buscemi opposite Seymour Cassel.

films.title: Delicatessen
films.note: Jean-Pierre Jeunet's post-apocalyptic import: 'Mad Max if it didn't go outside.'

films.title: Edward II
films.note: Derek Jarman's film, the new-queer-cinema standard-bearer of that class.

films.title: The Living End
films.note: Gregg Araki's road movie about two HIV-positive characters, unapologetic in a way My Own Private Idaho wasn't.

films.title: Swoon
films.note: Tom Kalin's Leopold-and-Loeb retelling, a companion piece to Todd Haynes's Poison.

films.title: Gas Food Lodging
films.note: Allison Anders's breakthrough; her career stalling out after it is the episode's case study in how indie Hollywood treats its women directors.

films.title: Poison Ivy
films.note: Katt Shea's Drew Barrymore vehicle, defended here as more than the punchline it became at the festival.

films.title: Streets
films.note: Shea's earlier film, with Christina Applegate as a drug-addicted sex worker in Los Angeles.

films.title: Mi Vida Loca
films.note: Anders's follow-up to Gas Food Lodging.

films.title: Grace of My Heart
films.note: Another Anders film; Ileana Douglas's performance is called one of the great unnominated turns of the mid-'90s.

films.title: Things Behind the Sun
films.note: Anders's last feature before an 11-year gap from directing.

films.title: River of Grass
films.note: Kelly Reichardt's debut two years later at Sundance, which also cost her nearly a decade before her next feature.

films.title: Kafka
films.note: Soderbergh's follow-up to sex, lies, and videotape, now largely forgotten and reportedly due for restoration.

films.title: sex, lies, and videotape
films.note: Cited as the first true Sundance phenomenon, three years ahead of Reservoir Dogs.

films.title: Mississippi Masala
films.note: Mira Nair's film from that year.

films.title: Brother's Keeper
films.note: Joe Berlinger and Bruce Sinofsky's documentary, a forerunner of the streaming true-crime boom.

films.title: Night on Earth
films.note: Jarmusch was already an established name by the time this played.

films.title: A Brief History of Time
films.note: Errol Morris's Stephen Hawking documentary.

films.title: Incident at Oglala
films.note: Michael Apted's documentary.

films.title: Color Adjustment
films.note: Marlon Riggs's history of Black identity on television, called ahead of its time and only now getting real distribution.

films.title: Zebrahead
films.note: Won the directing award that year; Michael Rapaport stars in an interracial romance.

films.title: The Grifters
films.note: Called a closer analog to Reservoir Dogs than the more overtly political Sundance films of the era.

films.title: City of Hope
films.note: John Sayles.

films.title: Poison
films.note: Todd Haynes.

films.title: American Dream
films.note: Barbara Kopple's documentary.

films.title: Roger & Me
films.note: Michael Moore, cited as the prior political touchstone before Reservoir Dogs.

films.title: Trees Lounge
films.note: Buscemi's directorial debut, called 'the platonic idea of a Sundance movie.'

films.title: Living in Oblivion
films.note: Tom DiCillo's navel-gazing-filmmaker comedy, another Buscemi vehicle.

films.title: Barton Fink
films.note: The Coens' navel-gazing-filmmaker predecessor.

films.title: King of New York
films.note: A small Buscemi part before Hollywood took notice of him.

films.title: Hurlyburly
films.note: Anthony Drazan's later, messier Sean Penn adaptation.

films.title: Four Rooms
films.note: The Sundance-kids-reunion movie.

films.title: Boogie Nights
films.note: New Line's attempt at 'their Pulp Fiction.'

films.title: Licorice Pizza
films.note: Cited as evidence of the Sundance-adjacent packaging that still surrounds Paul Thomas Anderson.

films.title: Hereditary
films.note: Traced as a root of Sundance's 'elevated horror' reputation.

films.title: Kaboom
films.note: Araki's film, revisited via his 2019 Starz series.

films.title: Speak No Evil
films.note: Teased at the very end as a nasty Danish horror pick from this year's festival.

Ads: Netflix FIFA World Cup Launch Edition

Once Upon a Time in Hollywood.title: Once Upon a Time... in Hollywood
